---
title: "About Me"
---

<style>
.about-page {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1.1fr 0.9fr;
  gap: 70px;
  align-items: start;
  margin-top: 35px;
}

.about-image {
  text-align: center;
}

.about-image img {
  width: 100%;
  height: auto;
  display: block;
}

.about-image figcaption {
  font-size: 0.75em;
  line-height: 1.35;
  color: #777777;
  text-align: center;
  margin-top: 8px;
}

.about-text {
  text-align: left;
  line-height: 1.6;
  color: #444444;
}

.about-text p {
  margin-bottom: 1.1em;
}

@media (max-width: 900px) {
  .about-page {
    grid-template-columns: 1fr;
    gap: 35px;
  }
}
</style>

<br/>

<div class="about-page">

<div class="about-image">

<figure>
<img src="Images/Cover_Picture.jpg" alt="Carlos during fieldwork in Chapada dos Veadeiros">
<figcaption>
Carlos in a _semprevivas_ field in Chapada dos Veadeiros National Park, Goiás, Brazil. Photo by Victor H. D. Silva.
</figcaption>
</figure>

<br/>
<br/>

<figure class="secondary-photo">
<img src="Images/Vellozia_seeds.jpg" alt="Carlos holding fruits and seeds of _Vellozia tubiflora_">
<figcaption>
Fruit and seeds of _Vellozia tubiflora_ (Velloziaceae), a widespread species in the Brazilian savannahs. Photo by Victor H. D. Silva.
</figcaption>
</figure>

<br/>
<br/>

<figure class="secondary-photo">
<img src="Images/Photo2.jpg" alt="Carlos during fieldwork in Chapada dos Veadeiros">
<figcaption>
Carlos during his first fieldwork in Chapada dos Veadeiros National Park. Photo by Yuri Souza.
</figcaption>
</figure>

</div>

<div class="about-text">

<p>
I am a Colombian seed scientist with a BSc in Ecology (2018) and a BSc in Biology (2019) from Pontificia Universidad Javeriana, in Bogotá, Colombia, and an MSc in Plant Biology (2022) from Universidade Federal de Minas Gerais, in Belo Horizonte, Brazil.
</p>

<p>
My interest in seed ecology began when I joined <a href="https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Sofia-Basto">Dr Sofía Basto’s</a> lab, where I worked on soil seed banks and germination studies. Under her supervision, I carried out my undergraduate research project on the temporal dynamics of seed rain in a tropical dry forest in the Magdalena River Valley, Colombia.
</p>

<p>
After completing my undergraduate studies, I moved to Brazil to pursue an MSc at the Centre of Ecological Synthesis and Conservation, UFMG, under the supervision of <a href="https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Fernando-Silveira-6">Dr Fernando A. O. Silveira</a>. Since then, I have deepened my focus on seed germination ecology while gaining expertise in quantitative synthesis and phylogenetic comparative methods. For my PhD, also at UFMG, I am combining quantitative synthesis and seed ecophysiology experiments to explore how different germination traits are coordinated with other traits commonly used to define plant ecological strategies.
</p>

<p>
My ultimate goal as a researcher is to build knowledge about the seeds of tropical ecosystems, with the hope that this information can contribute to their conservation and ecological restoration. Beyond these topics, I am interested in the decolonisation of science and in how scientific practices can become more inclusive and equitable. I have also ventured into <a href="https://caordonezparra.github.io/scicomm.html">science communication</a>, preparing pieces that engage broader audiences with seed science, and I am also enthusiastic about data visualisation.
</p>

<p>
If you would like to discuss or collaborate on any of these topics, please do not hesitate to <a href="https://caordonezparra.github.io/contact.html">contact me</a>!
</p>

</div>

</div>
